title: The Last Millennium of Chinese Imperial Rule and the Emergence of China's Modern Economy
abstract: This article presents a brief introduction to the relationship between the Chinese state c. tenth-early twentieth centuries and the emergence of China's modern economy that contains shared points of view with Dingxin Zhao's The Confucian-Legalist State: A New Theory of Chinese History as well as the identification of different key causal mechanisms to explain economic change.
